key_insight: Human feedback can steer synthetic data generation so a meta-learning model better matches an unobserved target distribution and generalizes more reliably under distribution shift.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery and other high-stakes scientific settings with sparse target data, GMHF offers a practical human-in-the-loop path to build robust surrogate models and reduce deployment risk.
method: Uses a theoretical generalization-error analysis to link performance to divergence between generated and target distributions.
Combines a Conditional Neural ODE as a generative digital twin with a reinforcement learning agent.
Iteratively updates latent physical parameters using expert feedback to align synthetic trajectories with human beliefs about target physics.
evidence_signal: On a nonlinear Duffing oscillator, the method lowers deployment loss as expert reliability increases, and divergence between generated and target data decreases under reliable feedback; results also extend to a non-dynamical probabilistic model.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and reliability of expert feedback, so noisy or inconsistent human input could limit gains and complicate deployment.
paper_title: Human-Machine Collaboration on Generative Meta-Learning: Model and Algorithm
